flashcards.Back: The remote pilot must report a qualifying accident to the FAA within $10$ days.
A report is required if property damage exceeds $500$ dollars, excluding the cost of the drone itself.
A report is required if an injury results in a Level $3$ severity or requires hospitalization.
If any person loses consciousness due to a drone accident, it must be reported within $10$ days.
Accident reports must be submitted electronically to the FAA via the FAADroneZone portal.
Property damage is calculated using the fair market value of repair or replacement.
The RPIC holds the ultimate authority and responsibility for the safe operation of the sUAS.
The RPIC may deviate from any Part $107$ rule to address an inflight emergency.
If an emergency deviation occurs, the RPIC must send a written report if requested by the FAA.
An inflight emergency is an unexpected event requiring immediate action by the RPIC to maintain safety.
The individual acting as the RPIC must be explicitly designated before the flight begins.
All drones weighing between $0.55$ lbs and $55$ lbs must be registered with the FAA.
A standard sUAS registration certificate is valid for exactly $3$ years.
The FAA-issued registration number must be clearly visible on the exterior surface of the drone.
Part $48$ establishes the official rules for the registration and marking of small unmanned aircraft.
Flying an unregistered drone can result in severe civil and criminal penalties.
A foreign-registered drone must be registered in the United States upon transfer of ownership.
If a registered drone is destroyed, the owner must update the FAA registration portal to cancel it.
A remote pilot must notify the FAA of any permanent change of mailing address within $30$ days.
A person must be at least $16$ years old to take the initial aeronautical knowledge test.
Passing the FAA aeronautical knowledge test is required to obtain a Remote Pilot Certificate.
Remote pilots must complete a recurrent online training course every $24$ calendar months to maintain currency.
Every applicant must pass a TSA security background check before a Remote Pilot Certificate is issued.
The remote pilot must be able to read, speak, write, and understand the English language.
The Remote Pilot Certificate must be easily accessible to present to the FAA or law enforcement upon request.
A pilot must be in a physical and mental condition to safely operate the sUAS.
Falsifying a certificate application will result in the denial, suspension, or revocation of the certificate.
The drone must remain within the unaided sight of the remote pilot or visual observer at all times.
Standard corrective lenses, such as glasses or contacts, are permitted to maintain visual line of sight.
A Visual Observer is an optional crew member who helps the RPIC maintain visual line of sight.
The RPIC and Visual Observer must maintain effective, constant communication during the entire flight.
Binoculars or telescopes cannot be used to satisfy the visual line of sight requirement.
A brief loss of visual line of sight is allowed for operational necessity but must be quickly regained.
The RPIC is required to ensure the drone is in a condition for safe operation before every flight.
The remote pilot must assess weather conditions, airspace restrictions, and ground hazards before flying.
The preflight inspection must ensure a proper communication link between the control station and the drone.
The pilot must verify that the battery is securely mounted and has sufficient charge for the flight.
The RPIC should follow all manufacturer guidelines and recommended maintenance procedures for the sUAS.
The maximum takeoff weight of the drone must be less than $55$ lbs, including any attached payload.
The maximum allowed altitude in uncontrolled airspace is $400$ feet Above Ground Level (AGL).
When flying over a structure, you may fly up to $400$ feet above its highest point within a $400$-foot radius.
The maximum allowable groundspeed for a small unmanned aircraft is $100$ mph or $87$ knots.
The minimum required weather visibility is $3$ statute miles from the control station.
The drone must remain at least $500$ feet below and $2,000$ feet horizontally from all clouds.
Flying at night requires the drone to be equipped with anti-collision lights visible for at least $3$ statute miles.
Civil twilight is $30$ minutes before sunrise or after sunset, requiring anti-collision lights for operations.
The small unmanned aircraft must always yield the right of way to all manned aircraft.
Flying over people is prohibited unless they are directly participating or located under a safe cover.
Operating a drone from a moving vehicle is prohibited unless flying over a sparsely populated area.
A pilot's blood alcohol concentration must be less than $0.04$ percent to legally operate a drone.
